reason: Taxi Driver’s Travis Bickle is a raw, unsettling portrait of urban alienation
Natural Born Killers’ violent duo fascinates even as you recoil from their spree
The Cook, The Thief… offers a grotesque study of venomous characters in a sumptuous cage
Goodfellas’ charismatic mobsters charm as their cruelty escalates
Cape Fear’s vengeful villain is magnetically repellent from first frame
Silence of the Lambs’ Hannibal Lecter is a masterclass in likable menace
Primal Fear’s Verbal Kint grips and disgusts in a layered, unreliable guise
The Usual Suspects’ Verbal Kint is repulsive yet riveting in every lie
Raging Bull’s Jake LaMotta manages raw empathy even as he brutalizes everyone
A Clockwork Orange’s Alex DeLarge keeps you entranced while you’re repulsed
